title: DLT Common Configuration Definitions
description: Shared definitions for dlt pipeline configurations

access_list.description: List of BigQuery access entries (group:email@domain.com, user:email@domain.com or serviceAccount:email@domain.com)

dataset_access_list.description: List of BigQuery dataset access entries. Supports IAM entries (ROLE:entity_type:email), authorized datasets (AUTHORIZED_DATASET:project.dataset), and authorized views (AUTHORIZED_VIEW:project.dataset.view)

write_disposition.description: How to write data to the destination table

merge_strategy.description: Strategy to use when write_disposition is 'merge'

replace_strategy.description: Strategy to use when write_disposition is 'replace'

dedup_sort.description: Sort order for deduplication (asc = oldest wins, desc = newest wins)

data_type.description: Data type (text, bigint, double, bool, timestamp, date, time, decimal, binary, complex, wei)

unique.description: Whether column values must be unique

primary_key.description: Whether column is part of primary key

default.description: Default value to use when column is NULL or empty (for handling SCD2 merge key nulls)

target_config.description: Common configuration for data loading and BigQuery destination

tags.description: Tags for filtering pipelines (e.g., daily, hourly, critical)

task_group.description: Optional group name for pipelines that should run together in the same Cloud Run task (e.g., 'livewrapped' to share authentication)

adapter.description: Pipeline adapter to use. Format: <namespace>.<path> (e.g., 'dlt_saga.api.genesyscloud', 'local.api.my_custom'). If omitted, falls back to folder-structure-based resolution.

access.description: BigQuery access control list (only applied in production)

table_format.description: Table format for BigQuery: 'native' for standard BigQuery tables, 'iceberg' for BigLake Iceberg tables

gcp_project_id.description: GCP project ID for BigQuery destination (defaults to config.toml or env var)

dataset_name.description: BigQuery dataset name (defaults to config.toml or env var)

primary_key.description: Primary key column(s) for merge operations

valid_from_column.description: Column name for SCD2 valid_from timestamp

valid_to_column.description: Column name for SCD2 valid_to timestamp

active_record_timestamp.description: Timestamp to use for active records in SCD2

boundary_timestamp.description: Boundary timestamp for SCD2 merge operations

row_version_column_name.description: Column name for row version tracking

deduplicate.description: Whether to deduplicate rows based on primary_key

dedup_sort.description: Sort order for deduplication when deduplicate is true

dedup_sort_column.description: Column to use for deduplication sorting

hard_delete_column.description: Column indicating rows should be hard deleted

partition_column.description: Column to partition BigQuery table by (must be DATE or TIMESTAMP)

cluster_columns.description: Columns to cluster BigQuery table by (max 4)

columns.description: Column type hints for explicit schema definition. Keys can be either the original source column name (e.g., 'Oppty_TargetAmount') or the dlt-normalized snake_case name (e.g., 'oppty_target_amount'). dlt normalizes identifiers by splitting CamelCase on word boundaries: 'TargetAmount' becomes 'target_amount', not 'targetamount'. Using a key that matches neither form silently creates a ghost column in the destination with all NULL values.
